_title: NFPA 70 Chapter 4 — Equipment for General Use
_note: Light Ch 4 articles inline. Arts 408 (panelboards), 430 (motors), 450 (transformers) have dedicated per-article files — pointers only.

articles.scope: Flexible cord types (Table 400.4), uses permitted (400.10), uses NOT permitted (400.12 — common violation source).

key_sections.title: Uses Permitted
key_sections.summary: Pendants, fixtures, portable equipment, elevator cables, hoist/crane, prevention of transmission of noise/vibration, removable equipment, identified appliances

key_sections.title: Uses Not Permitted
key_sections.summary: Substitute for fixed wiring, run through walls/floors/ceilings/doors, attached to building surfaces, concealed, in raceways (with limited exceptions)

common_errors: Using SO/SJOOW flexible cord as fixed wiring — prohibited by 400.12(1) and frequent inspection failure
articles.divergence_notes: NEC restricts flexible cord use much more tightly than IEC; IEC permits flexible cables in fixed wiring with conditions.
articles.usage_notes: Read 400.12 first. If the application doesn't match 400.10 exactly, it's prohibited.

articles.scope: Snap switches, dimmers, transfer switches (≤1000 V) — installation, position, ratings, identification.

key_sections.title: Switches Controlling Lighting Loads
key_sections.summary: Grounded conductor (neutral) required at switch box for control devices needing one (most occupancy sensors, dimmers); exceptions for switch loops in existing dwellings

key_sections.title: Provisions for General-Use Snap Switches
key_sections.summary: Switches mounted in metal boxes must be effectively grounded

key_sections.title: Rating and Use of Snap Switches
key_sections.summary: Switch ratings — AC general-use (40A 277V), AC-DC general-use, motor circuit (Type V), heater

common_errors: Routing a switch loop without the neutral conductor in new construction — 404.2(C) requires neutral at virtually every switch box
articles.divergence_notes: NEC 404.2(C) neutral-at-switch-box rule (added in 2011) has no IEC equivalent; IEC does not require neutral at every switch.
articles.usage_notes: Specify neutral at every switch box on new-construction drawings unless one of the four exceptions in 404.2(C) clearly applies.

articles.article_title: Receptacles, Cord Connectors, and Attachment Plugs
articles.scope: Receptacle ratings, types (15A/20A/30A/50A configurations per NEMA WD-6), GFCI/AFCI compliance via the receptacle, tamper-resistant (TR) requirements (406.12), weather-resistant (WR) (406.9), countertop placement.

key_sections.title: Receptacles in Damp or Wet Locations
key_sections.summary: Damp = listed for damp/wet; wet = listed for wet + cover that maintains weatherproof when plug inserted (in-use cover)

key_sections.title: Tamper-Resistant Receptacles (TR)
key_sections.summary: All 125 V and 250 V, 15A/20A receptacles in dwelling units, child care, preschool, classroom, waiting rooms, hotels, dormitories MUST be tamper-resistant

common_errors: Specifying standard (non-TR) receptacles in dwelling units — 406.12 requires TR in every receptacle outlet
articles.divergence_notes: NEC TR receptacle requirement (406.12) has no IEC equivalent; IEC relies on socket-design standards (BS 1363 shutters, Schuko safety, etc.) and does not have a separate 'tamper-resistant' classification.
articles.usage_notes: Default to TR on all dwelling-unit outlets; default to WR + in-use cover for any outdoor receptacle.

articles.scope: Luminaire installation — locations (wet/damp), in clothes closets (410.16), above bathtubs (410.10(D)), recessed (410.110-130), poles (410.30).

key_sections.title: Luminaires in Bathtub and Shower Areas
key_sections.summary: No luminaire within 3 ft horizontal and 8 ft vertical of bathtub rim/shower stall threshold unless wet-listed

key_sections.title: Luminaires in Clothes Closets
key_sections.summary: Permitted types/locations — surface fluorescent/LED on wall or ceiling outside storage volume OK; incandescent prohibited within storage zone

key_sections.title: Recessed Luminaires (Part XII)
key_sections.summary: Type IC for direct contact with insulation; Type non-IC requires 3-in clearance from insulation; thermal protection per UL 1598

common_errors: Specifying non-IC recessed luminaires in insulated ceiling — fire hazard; use IC-rated for direct contact
articles.divergence_notes: NEC bathtub zone (3 ft × 8 ft per 410.10(D)) differs from IEC 60364-7-701 zones (Zone 0, 1, 2 with specific dimensions and IP requirements). NEC has no explicit IPX rating in the bath zone — relies on 'damp' / 'wet' listing.
articles.usage_notes: On bath/shower designs, default to wet-listed luminaires in the 3-ft × 8-ft envelope.

articles.scope: Fastened-in-place and cord-connected appliances — branch-circuit sizing (422.10), disconnect (422.30+), GFCI for specific appliances (422.5: vending machines, drinking fountains, dishwashers, etc.).

key_sections.title: GFCI Protection for Personnel
key_sections.summary: Listed appliances requiring GFCI (per their listing) — vending machines, drinking fountains, dishwashers, etc.

key_sections.title: Branch-Circuit Rating
key_sections.summary: Individual branch circuit ≥ 125% of nameplate for continuous-duty appliance; ≥ 100% for non-continuous

key_sections.title: Disconnecting Means — General
key_sections.summary: Each appliance must have a disconnect

common_errors: Sizing appliance circuit at 100% of nameplate when appliance is continuous-duty — must be 125% per 422.10(A)(1)
articles.divergence_notes: NEC 125% continuous-duty multiplier is explicit and prescriptive; IEC handles via cable derating + circuit-rated-In selection.
articles.usage_notes: Always confirm continuous-duty vs non-continuous before sizing the branch circuit.

articles.scope: Hermetic refrigerant motor-compressor branch circuits — rated-load current (RLA), short-circuit ground-fault protection, conductor sizing (440.32-33), disconnect within sight (440.14).

key_sections.title: Location of Disconnect
key_sections.summary: Disconnect within sight from and readily accessible to the AC equipment

key_sections.title: Application and Selection of Short-Circuit Ground-Fault Protection
key_sections.summary: OCPD selection — up to 175% of RLA + branch-circuit selection current (BCSC); 225% if 175% trips on start

key_sections.title: Single Motor-Compressor
key_sections.summary: Conductor ampacity ≥ 125% of RLA or branch-circuit selection current (whichever is greater)

common_errors: Using full-load amps (FLA) from a generic motor table for a hermetic compressor — Art 440 requires RLA from the equipment nameplate
related_iec_60364: IEC 60364-5-55 Clause 559 (Luminaires and lighting installations) — peripheral relevance only
articles.divergence_notes: Art 440 is NEC-specific: the 'hermetic refrigerant motor-compressor' construct and the RLA/BCSC dual-rating system have no IEC equivalent; IEC sizes via standard motor-circuit rules (similar to NEC Art 430).
articles.usage_notes: Always size from nameplate RLA, not generic FLA. Confirm disconnect is within sight (most common AC inspection failure).

articles.scope: Storage batteries (lead-acid, alkaline, lithium-ion) for stationary applications — battery rooms, ventilation, disconnect, charging.

key_sections.title: Battery Locations
key_sections.summary: Ventilation per 480.10, working space, racks, spill containment for flooded lead-acid

key_sections.title: Direct-Current Disconnect
key_sections.summary: DC disconnect required; lockable in OFF position

common_errors: Treating Art 480 as the only battery rule for ESS — for ESS systems ≥1 kWh, Art 706 (Energy Storage Systems) applies in addition
articles.divergence_notes: Art 480 covers stationary lead-acid + alkaline + Li-ion; Art 706 (Energy Storage Systems) layered on top for ESS. IEC handles via IEC 62485-2 (separate standard) referenced from IEC 60364.
articles.usage_notes: For ESS designs, read 480 + 706 together. UPS systems generally fall under 480 alone.

articles.scope: Panelboard ratings, classification, 42-circuit rule, spacings, working clearances.
Motor circuit conductor sizing, OCPD, overload, disconnect, MCC.
Transformer overcurrent protection, primary/secondary, vault, ventilation.
